entities.name: IoOpenDeviceRegistryKey
entities.description: Opens a registry key for device-specific configuration information.

entities.name: IoOpenDriverRegistryKey
entities.description: Opens a driver-specific registry key with a specified DRIVER_REGKEY_TYPE, such as DriverRegKeySharedPersistentState

entities.name: GetServiceRegistryStateKey
entities.description: Retrieves the registry key handle for the service state information

entities.name: SERVICE_REGISTRY_STATE_TYPE
entities.description: Specifies types of service registry states. Registry values supplied by the INF in the "Parameters" subkey should be treated as read-only. Override values should be written into Internal or Shared service registry state. Settings should be first checked in mutable state, then in immutable state if not found.

entities.name: WdfDriverOpenPersistentStateRegistryKey
entities.description: The WdfDriverOpenPersistentStateRegistryKey function opens a registry key where the driver can store persistent state information.

entities.name: GetSharedServiceRegistryStateKey
entities.description: Retrieves the shared service registry state key for Win32 services

entities.name: IoGetDeviceDirectory
entities.description: Retrieves the device directory object for a specified device object. Used in WDM (Windows Driver Model) drivers.

entities.name: IoGetDriverDirectory
entities.description: Retrieves the path of a driver's directory. Used in WDM and KMDF drivers with DirectoryType parameter specifying directory type.

entities.name: GetServiceDirectory
entities.description: Retrieves the directory path for service-related files. When called with eDirectoryType parameter set to ServiceDirectoryPersistentState, it returns the persistent state directory. Services must expose proper interfaces (like COM/RPC for Win32 services or IOCTL for drivers) if they want to allow modification of these settings by other components.

entities.name: WdfDeviceQueryInterfaceProperty
entities.description: Retrieves the specified device interface property.

entities.name: WdfDeviceAllocAndQueryInterfaceProperty
entities.description: Allocates memory and retrieves an interface property for a WDF device

entities.name: WdfDeviceAssignInterfaceProperty
entities.description: Windows 8.1) WdfDeviceRetrieveDeviceDirectoryString

entities.name: REG_SZ
entities.description: A null-terminated string in the Windows Registry. This is one of the predefined registry value types.

entities.name: DRIVER_ISOLATION_VIOLATION
entities.description: Driver Isolation Violation error code. Indicates registry operations should only use key handles returned from WDF or WDM APIs. Detected creation of unisolated registry key \REGISTRY\MACHINE\SYSTEM\SomeKeyThatShouldNotExist. Consider running Device Fundamentals tests with DV driver isolation checks enabled on your driver to help catch driver isolation violations early.

entities.name: WDF_DEVICE_INTERFACE_PROPERTY_DATA
entities.description: Structure containing device interface property data in Windows Driver Framework (WDF)

entities.name: WDF_DEVICE_INTERFACE_PROPERTY_DATA_INIT
entities.description: Initializes a WDF_DEVICE_INTERFACE_PROPERTY_DATA structure with device interface GUID and property key

entities.name: section
entities.description: An INF file section type in Windows used for driver installation/uninstallation. Beginning with Windows 10 version 1903, architecture-decorated sections can enable primitive driver functionality.

entities.name: NT_TARGET_VERSION
entities.description: Specifies the target Windows version for driver development. Set to the lowest version your driver supports (e.g. Windows 10). Determines compatibility with multiple Windows versions.

entities.name: PWTS_SESSION_INFO
entities.description: Pointer to a WTS_SESSION_INFO structure that contains information about a terminal services session.

entities.name: WTSFreeMemory
entities.description: Frees memory allocated by a Terminal Services function.

entities.name: STAMPINF_VERSION
entities.description: Environment variable used when building drivers. Enables the date stamp in INF DriverVer directive. If enabled, Driver Version Directive Date must be specified.

entities.name: WDTF_TARGETS
entities.description: WDTF (Windows Driver Test Framework) targets collection for querying device targets

entities.name: WDTF_TEST
entities.description: Windows Driver Test Framework (WDTF) test structure

entities.name: WDTF_TARGET
entities.description: Windows Device Testing Framework (WDTF) target object interface for device testing scenarios
